info.title: nugraph
info.description: Generates dependency graphs for .NET projects and NuGet packages.

options.name: --direction
options.description: The direction of the dependency graph, possible values are [b]LeftToRight[/] (recommended for large graphs) and [b]TopToBottom[/] (good for small graphs)

options.name: --format
options.description:
The format to use when the [b]--output[/] option is not specified
Use [b]mmd[/] or [b]mermaid[/] for Mermaid Live Editor https://mermaid.live
Use [b]dot[/], [b]gv[/] or [b]graphviz[/] for Edotor https://edotor.net
See https://github.com/0xced/nugraph/#output for even more supported formats

options.name: --framework
options.description:
The target framework to consider when building the dependency graph
See https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/standard/frameworks#supported-target-frameworks for the list of supported target frameworks

options.name: --ignore
options.description: Packages to ignore in the dependency graph, may be used multiple times and supports * wildcards, e.g. [b]--ignore "System.*"[/]

options.name: --include-version
options.description: Include package versions in the dependency graph nodes, e.g. [b]Serilog/4.3.0[/] instead of [b]Serilog[/]

options.name: --log
options.description: The NuGet operations log level, possible values are [b]Debug[/], [b]Verbose[/], [b]Information[/], [b]Minimal[/], [b]Warning[/] and [b]Error[/]

options.name: --no-links
options.description:
Remove clickable links from the the dependency graph
Can be useful to reduce the size of the graph if it becomes too large and Mermaid Live Editor is returning "Maximum text size in diagram exceeded"

options.name: --output
options.description:
The path to the dependency graph output file
A Mermaid diagram is written if the file name ends with either [b].mmd[/] or [b].mermaid[/], otherwise a Graphviz diagram is written
If not specified, the default web browser is used to render the dependency graph using an online service
See also the [b]--format[/] and [b]--url[/] options

options.name: --runtime
options.description:
The target runtime to consider when building the dependency graph
See https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/core/rid-catalog#known-rids for the list of known runtime identifiers

options.name: --title
options.description: The title of the dependency graph

options.name: --url
options.description:
Defines what to do when the [b]--output[/] option is not specified
Use [b]open[/] (the default) for rendering the dependency graph using an online service or [b]print[/] for printing the graph URL on the standard output without opening the browser
Both options can be combined with a comma, i.e. [b]open,print[/] to both open the URL and print it

arguments.name: source
arguments.description:
The source of the graph
Can be either
  * the name of a NuGet package, optionally with a specific version, e.g. [b]Newtonsoft.Json/13.0.3[/]
  * a .NET project file (csproj/fsproj/vbproj)
  * a directory containing a single .NET project
